description: Audit personal devpods dotfiles repo and strategically consolidate useful components into DotfilesManagerMac with recursive, scalable implementation

userStories.title: Audit personal dotfiles repo structure and contents
userStories.description: As a project maintainer, I want to understand the contents of my personal dotfiles so I can identify reusable components.
acceptanceCriteria: Document directory structure of https://github.com/santiagobermudezparra/dotfiles
List all dot_config/* subdirectories and their purpose
List all scripts/* scripts and their purpose
List shell aliases, functions, and profile configuration
Identify patterns: chezmoi templates, script conventions, etc.
Create .planning/DOTFILES_AUDIT.md with complete findings
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Map components to DotfilesManagerMac categories
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want to prioritize which personal dotfiles components to consolidate based on value and fit.
acceptanceCriteria: Categorize each audit finding: (1) Directly applicable, (2) Requires adaptation, (3) Out of scope
Document rationale for each categorization
Include implementation difficulty estimate (easy, medium, hard)
Prioritize by value: core dev tools → nice-to-haves
Create .planning/CONSOLIDATION_ROADMAP.md with phased approach
Map components to specific stories below
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integrate tmux configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tmux configured for efficient Mac/Neovim workflows, improving window and pane management.
acceptanceCriteria: Review tmux config from personal dotfiles (dot_tmux.conf)
Adapt for Mac keybindings and Neovim integration
Add to DotfilesManagerMac as dot_tmux.conf
Include sensible defaults: prefix key, navigation, window naming
Update bootstrap to create ~/.tmux.conf symlink
Document tmux setup and key bindings in README
Test tmux + Neovim integration (split panes, window nav)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Consolidate shell aliases and functions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want useful shell aliases and functions available in my corporate Mac environment.
acceptanceCriteria: Review dot_zshrc from personal dotfiles
Identify dev-workflow aliases (git shortcuts, sf CLI helpers, etc.)
Filter out: personal tools, internal company references, non-Mac specific
Adapt remaining aliases for DotfilesManagerMac context
Add to dot_zshrc in DotfilesManagerMac (or new dot_zsh_custom if needed)
Document new aliases in README 'Shell Aliases' section
Test aliases work in both interactive and scripted contexts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Consolidate reusable scripts to bin/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want utility scripts available that support Neovim, Apex, and Salesforce workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: List all scripts from personal dotfiles scripts/ directory
Identify which support Neovim, Apex, or Salesforce development
Adapt selected scripts for Mac and DotfilesManagerMac context
Add to bin/ directory in DotfilesManagerMac
Update bootstrap to add bin/ to PATH during chezmoi apply
Document each script's purpose in README 'Scripts' section
Test scripts work after chezmoi apply
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update .gitignore for recursive Ralph implementation
userStories.description: As a future contributor, I want .gitignore configured to support recurring Ralph phases without re-configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Add .planning/prd.json (current Ralph PRD)
Add .planning/progress.txt (Ralph progress tracking)
Add .planning/archive/ (archived completed PRDs)
Add .planning/DEBUG_*.md (debug artifacts from phases)
Add .planning/AUDIT_*.md and *_ROADMAP.md (audit outputs)
Add *.swp, *.swo, *~, .DS_Store (editor/OS temporaries)
Verify .planning/ directory itself is tracked (for new PRDs)
Document strategy in README 'Extensibility' section
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Partially completed in PRD prep; this story completes it

userStories.title: Document consolidation strategy and extensibility
userStories.description: As a future contributor, I want to understand how to extend DotfilesManagerMac with new features using Ralph.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Extensibility & Future PRDs' section to README
Explain .planning/ directory structure and purpose
Document how to create new Ralph PRDs for this project
Provide PRD template (reference PRDS.md structure)
Link to DOTFILES_AUDIT.md and CONSOLIDATION_ROADMAP.md for context
Include example: 'Adding a new feature: create prd-N.json, run Ralph'
Explain branch naming and merge strategy
Typecheck passes
